reviews.review_description: When I first bought it, I could not select save or even get it to work so I decided   to get a refund. Then I went looking for another and all I could find was Gadgeteer, which was not fun at all.  Then after reading positive and negative reviews I bought it again, and now I have learned to use it properly and would like to say it is a lor of fun. No complaints from me. It saves my creations and loads them out correctly. No glitches just a lot of un!
The game is so fun and my friend may be getting it, so if you can add multiplayer that would be awesome! Plus the game is really easy to learn what every thing does
This app is the cats pajamas. I'm 55 years old and this app is a dream that for most of my life, I never thought would come true. Rube Goldberg gew gaw.

reviews.review_title: Not able to load saved scenarios
reviews.review_description:
Almost useless if i am not able to continune a machine that i have saved. not able to show it to soneone later on.
This functionality does NOT work. i do not seem to be the only person experiencing this so think before you buy.
sometimes when loading a machine it apparently is working as i can hear the sounds of things that are moving but machine is not visible.
Definitely asking for a refund.

❗️❗️❌️HUGE WASTE OF TIME AND MONEY, DO NOT BUY❌️❗️❗️I spent 4 HOURS building and testing this elaborate thing that goes through all this stuff in my room just to "save the game" and get off for the night... MY MISTAKE I load up my save from last night just to see half of the pieces missing, I made 3 separate saves so it was NOT my fault, each save had different parts missing, parts floating In the air and the real kicker, I spent an hour trying to fix this just to have the entire game turn into one huge bug, I would try to grab rails and move them just for the entire 12 foot rail to LITERALLY FLOAT AWAY I've tried clicking undo, reseting my save, reseting my game, reseting my oculus, I've tried everything and all just to be pissed off I spent 6 hours on this game along with $10 what a waste of my money, I should have got "among us" or literally ANYTHING else, the devs don't care about making their game better and run smoothly, hours wasted I will never purchase any games or anything by this terrible game designer and I hope you wouldn't either
Broken. All I have to say

Hard to press buttons, select objects, etc
Basically unplayable, but maybe I just don't understand it.
the tutorial needs to cover basic contols better

Seems to be the only way to talk to anyone from this company is here.  So I'm just gonna put it right here that your Patrick Mahomes football game is broken.   The game goes black screen as soon as you score a touchdown.  Please fix it!

The car track, the launchers the rockets.  The Dominos ... Anyone one of these a fun creative game in itself.  Nearly endless options (and truly endless potential). Creative intelligent people will love this.  This is one of the few games I bought minutes after hearing about it.  

The Physics is intuitive and forgiving.   I love the Alien Anti Gravity thingie...

Well worth the $.  Endless playability.  Fun!

developer_response.body: So glad you are enjoing it!

reviews.review_description:
Just purchased this for quest 3 but I hear no sound/ui sfx or music. Game looks fun but don't feel like playing much when there is no sound.
Edit:After update sound is working, changed to 5 Star rating. Game is great if you are creative with building stuff.

This game is so much fun but I get so frustrated playing it because it glitches so much. Please update or help. If you reach out or help I will get more into detail. And don’t try to get me in some kind of discord chat cause mine isn’t working right now.

developer_response.body:
We just made an update today which should help with some issues we've seen. 
If you have more details that is always helpful. Another thing to look for is keeping your workspace the same from from day to day. Because we use the Meta Quest anchoring system, the saved machines are looking to be in the precise location that they were build before.

reviews.review_description: I need a refund ASAP

developer_response.body:
Hi Casin055, 
Oh no! You can't enjoy a game you can't play. Without any details it is difficult to know what went wrong. Assuming you are on a Quest 2, Quest Pro or Quest 3, if should work. If you are willing to try, you can uninstall it, turn off your Quest and restart it, and reinstall the App. If that doesn't work you can get a refund through your purchase history on the Meta Quest app.

developer_response.body:
Hi Rygoto, 
Oh no! You can't enjoy a game you can't play. Without any details it is difficult to know what went wrong. Assuming you are on a Quest 2, Quest Pro or Quest 3, if should work. If you are willing to try, you can uninstall it, turn off your Quest and restart it, and reinstall the App.

reviews.review_description: This game would be super fun and addicting if it worked. Unfortunately, nothing saves and it constantly glitches. I keep wasting my time making awesome contraptions only to have them not save or worse, shift and stop working or have parts disappear. Instead of being enjoyable it’s just frustrating. I wish it worked.

developer_response.body:
I'm sorry things are not working out the way you'd hoped. We are coming out with an update soon that may address some issues on some devices. 
But, there is a 'save machine' feature in the virtual tablet, that should allow you to save any machine you make. 
One important note: the Rube Goldberg Machines you make are 'tied' to specific 'anchors' in the location that they are first made. So you can't make a machine in one room, take your headset to another room, and load the same machine. This is done to ensure each save keeps the precise relative locations of all the objects as you left them. 
Another thing to make sure is that your room setup is done correctly. That is managed by the Quest software itself, our app just sends you to it. 
 Let use know if either of those things helps.

reviews.review_description: Love this game with pass through. hours of endless fun. Great Physics only improvement could be how items are gripped. Still a five star.

developer_response.body: Thanks. Quick question. Do you use controller or hands? You might try to other and find you have a better experience.

reviews.review_title: I demand a refund. this is useless.
reviews.review_description: this is not what you think it is. there is nothing you can turn on those automatic to ready to play there's nothing ready to try there's no demos to actually use its useless if you don't know what you're doing but they don't teach you.

developer_response.body:
Hello,
So sorry you haven't enjoyed the app. Your experience isn't what we expect or have seen. If you are on a Quest 2 or Quest Pro it should work as intended (sorry it won't work on Quest 1). There is a guided tutorial the first time you launch the game, and you can get to the tutorial at any time by goign to the options menu in on the tablet. 
It is also important to follow the instructions to set up the mixed-realty passthrough (which is handled by the Quest software,nto ours). 
The game is intended to be a sandbox. We don't tell you what machines to make, or how to make them. Inspired by Rube Goldberg we want you to come up with your own crazy machines. 
(Note: we are looking into adding some specific challenges in the future).
I hope you give it another try.If you still are not satisfied. You can get a refund by requesting a return on the Meta Quest mobile app:

1. Open the Meta Quest mobile app on your phone.
2. Tap Store, then select Quest 2 on the top left.
3. Tap Menu at the bottom, then tap Settings.
4. Tap Purchase History, then tap the app you want to request a refund for.
5. Tap Request Refund.
6. Follow the on-screen instructions to request your refund.
Thanks for trying us!

reviews.review_description: I purchased this game and I cannot even get started in 3 weeks or more because I tapped on Tutorial to see how to play the Game it’s not activating. I would prefer a refund

developer_response.body: We've updated the game to address a bug where the UI wasn't allowing some users access to the tutorial. I hope you give us another chance.

reviews.review_description:
The game astutely utilises room-scale + video passthrough technology, allowing players to construct intricate, physics-defying machines right in their living rooms, without any real-world mess to tidy up afterwards. 

Whether you're a tinkerer, a gamer, or a fan of innovative technology, This sets an accessible new standard for mixed reality gaming, delivering a unique and gratifying experience that will have you lost in the intricate mechanics of whimsical contraptions for hours on end.

Note: Please Provide built in machines that showcases the substantial devices available - this will help accelerate intricate gameplay.

reviews.review_title: WOW... This is what VR/AR was made for!
reviews.review_description: I saw this released today, and being a fan of Incredible Machines many moons ago, I thought this looked rather interesting. I convinced myself that my son would like this, as he is ill off school this week, so will buy it as a gift for him tomorrow to explore, but thought I should have a little try first, so I can help him with the controls and learning the ropes....well, 3 hours later, my Quest ran out of batteries - I was having so much fun.  I love that they incorporated handtracking, though some improvement could be made in that area, as I ended up using controllers due to feeling like an angry pinching crab trying to grab objects that would not register... Aside from that, the controls felt natural, physics great, and possibilities endless. Looking forward to seeing some more objects to interact with (don't get me wrong, there are plenty, but more is always better in this case (treadmills?, electric circuits?, fire? ropes? water?) - But for a game that has JUST launched, this is well wroth the price of admission. One of my top 5 games, and I have maaaaany. Definitely a BUY!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, your review means a lot to us! And your feedback is appreciated, especially as we evaluate expanding the game.

reviews.review_title: Endless toys, none of the cleanup
reviews.review_description:
5 year old loves playing just to place the tracks and cars, but I like making more and I do

Devs, if you’re interested in expanding this to multiplayer, checkout how the devs of Spatial Ops implemented shared space (SideQuest.)

developer_response.body: Multiplayer is on our roadmap! Stay tuned.
